steps.title: Isolate a
steps.content: Starting from $x = 6a(b+11)$, divide both sides by $6(b+11)$ (nonzero since $b$ is positive): $$a = \frac{x}{6(b+11)}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A gives $a = \frac{x}{6} - (b+11)$, which would only follow if the original equation were $x = 6(a + b + 11)$ — not the given product form.
  • B gives $a = \frac{x}{6(b+11)}$, exactly what we derived.
  • C gives $a = \frac{6(b+11)}{x}$, the reciprocal of B and not equivalent.
  • D gives $a = 6x(b+11)$, which would multiply rather than divide by $6(b+11)$.
That is the correct answer.
